_status: machine-drafted v2 scheme, unreviewed — review pass + Aaron line-item pending

playbook_grounding.position: The DPA's covered-activity perimeter and vendor authority should keep personal-data work under the customer's documented instructions and the agreed services.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Vendor will process personal data only on Customer's documented instructions and only as necessary to provide the services or comply with applicable law.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if the vendor can act outside documented customer directions.

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Vendor will process personal data only on Customer's documented instructions and only as necessary to provide the services or comply with applicable law.

acceptable.text: Vendor will process personal data only on Customer's documented instructions and as necessary to provide the services; administrative notice of the processing is due within 5 days. Vendor updates the instruction log every 15 days and supplies deletion confirmation within 3 days after completion.

Vendor may process personal data outside a service instruction only to the extent applicable law requires and will notify Customer, where legally permitted, within 5 days. Vendor updates the instruction log every 30 days and supplies deletion confirmation within 12 days after completion.

Vendor will process personal data only on Customer's documented instructions and as necessary to provide the services; administrative notice of the processing is due within 10 days. Vendor updates the instruction log every 45 days and supplies deletion confirmation within 9 days after completion.

Vendor may process personal data outside a service instruction only to the extent applicable law requires and will notify Customer, where legally permitted, within 3 days. Vendor updates the instruction log every 60 days and supplies deletion confirmation within 6 days after completion.

unacceptable.text: Vendor may process personal data outside a service instruction only to the extent applicable law requires and will notify Customer, where legally permitted, within 10 days. Vendor updates the instruction log every 30 days and supplies deletion confirmation within 9 days after completion.

Vendor may process personal data outside Customer's instructions when Vendor considers the activity operationally useful and will notify Customer within 5 days. Vendor updates the instruction log every 15 days and supplies deletion confirmation within 12 days after completion.

Vendor may process personal data outside Customer's instructions when Vendor considers the activity operationally useful and will notify Customer within 15 days. Vendor updates the instruction log every 60 days and supplies deletion confirmation within 3 days after completion.

Vendor may process personal data outside a service instruction only to the extent applicable law requires and will notify Customer, where legally permitted, within 7 days. Vendor updates the instruction log every 45 days and supplies deletion confirmation within 6 days after completion.

playbook_grounding.position: Use boundaries should prevent vendor reuse for unrelated analytics, product development, benchmarking, or model training.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Vendor will not use personal data for its own analytics, benchmarking, product development, model training, or other independent business purposes.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if the vendor can repurpose protected material for independent business benefit.

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Vendor will not use personal data for its own analytics, benchmarking, product development, model training, or other independent business purposes.

acceptable.text: Vendor may use the data only for documented Customer-directed service analytics and may not reuse it for Vendor's independent benefit. The information remains personal data. Service-analytics working files are retained for 30 days, and anonymization controls are reviewed every 6 months.

Vendor may use the data only for documented Customer-directed service analytics and may not reuse it for Vendor's independent benefit. Before any independent use, the information must be irreversibly anonymized so it is no longer personal data. Service-analytics working files are retained for 45 days, and anonymization controls are reviewed every 3 months.

Vendor may use the resulting information for independent analytics, benchmarking, product development, and model improvement. Before any independent use, the information must be irreversibly anonymized so it is no longer personal data. Service-analytics working files are retained for 60 days, and anonymization controls are reviewed every 12 months.

Vendor may use the data only for documented Customer-directed service analytics and may not reuse it for Vendor's independent benefit. The information remains personal data. Service-analytics working files are retained for 15 days, and anonymization controls are reviewed every 9 months.

unacceptable.text: Vendor may use the resulting information for independent analytics, benchmarking, product development, and model improvement. The information remains personal data. Service-analytics working files are retained for 45 days, and anonymization controls are reviewed every 12 months.

Vendor may use the resulting information for independent analytics, benchmarking, product development, and model improvement. The information is pseudonymized but remains linkable to individuals. Service-analytics working files are retained for 30 days, and anonymization controls are reviewed every 3 months.

Vendor may use the data only for documented Customer-directed service analytics and may not reuse it for Vendor's independent benefit. Vendor may combine the personal data with other customer datasets. Service-analytics working files are retained for 15 days, and anonymization controls are reviewed every 6 months.

Vendor may use the resulting information for independent analytics, benchmarking, product development, and model improvement. The information remains personal data. Service-analytics working files are retained for 60 days, and anonymization controls are reviewed every 9 months.

playbook_grounding.position: Downstream processors should require customer approval, written pass-through commitments, and vendor responsibility for their acts and omissions.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Vendor may use subprocessors only with Customer's prior approval or the agreed notice-and-objection process, must bind each subprocessor to written obligations at least as protective as this DPA, and remains liable for subprocessor performance.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if downstream providers can handle protected data without approval controls, equivalent written duties, or vendor responsibility.

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Vendor may use subprocessors only with Customer's prior approval or the agreed notice-and-objection process, must bind each subprocessor to written obligations at least as protective as this DPA, and remains liable for subprocessor performance.

acceptable.text: Vendor may appoint a subprocessor through Customer prior approval; Vendor must impose written duties at least as protective as this DPA and remains liable for subprocessor performance. Vendor responds to a substantiated objection within 12 days and reviews subprocessor compliance every 9 months.

Vendor may appoint a subprocessor through the agreed notice-and-objection process on 30 days’ advance notice; Vendor must impose written duties at least as protective as this DPA and remains liable for subprocessor performance. Vendor responds to a substantiated objection within 20 days and reviews subprocessor compliance every 6 months.

Vendor may appoint a subprocessor through the agreed notice-and-objection process on 45 days’ advance notice; Vendor must impose written duties at least as protective as this DPA and remains liable for subprocessor performance. Vendor responds to a substantiated objection within 5 days and reviews subprocessor compliance every 3 months.

Vendor may appoint a subprocessor through Customer prior approval; Vendor must impose written duties at least as protective as this DPA and remains liable for subprocessor performance. Vendor responds to a substantiated objection within 10 days and reviews subprocessor compliance every 12 months.

unacceptable.text: Vendor may appoint a subprocessor through the agreed notice-and-objection process on 15 days’ advance notice; Vendor must impose written duties at least as protective as this DPA and remains liable for subprocessor performance. Vendor responds to a substantiated objection within 20 days and reviews subprocessor compliance every 3 months.

Vendor may appoint a subprocessor through the agreed notice-and-objection process on 30 days’ advance notice; Vendor need not impose equivalent written data-protection duties, although it remains the contracting party. Vendor responds to a substantiated objection within 12 days and reviews subprocessor compliance every 6 months.

Vendor may appoint a subprocessor through Customer prior approval; Vendor need not impose equivalent written data-protection duties, although it remains the contracting party. Vendor responds to a substantiated objection within 10 days and reviews subprocessor compliance every 9 months.

Vendor may appoint a subprocessor through the agreed notice-and-objection process on 20 days’ advance notice; Vendor must impose written duties at least as protective as this DPA and remains liable for subprocessor performance. Vendor responds to a substantiated objection within 5 days and reviews subprocessor compliance every 12 months.

playbook_grounding.position: Operational support should require reasonable assistance with data subject requests and regulator-facing inquiries involving the services.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Vendor will reasonably assist Customer with data subject requests, regulator inquiries, and privacy assessments relating to the services, taking into account the nature of processing and information available to Vendor.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if the customer lacks practical support for rights requests or official inquiries.

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Vendor will reasonably assist Customer with data subject requests, regulator inquiries, and privacy assessments relating to the services, taking into account the nature of processing and information available to Vendor.

acceptable.text: Vendor will reasonably assist Customer with each data-subject request relating to the services, taking account of the processing and information available to Vendor, and will begin the requested support within 10 days. Customer may group up to 40 related requests in one intake, and Vendor provides status updates every 8 days while work remains open.

Vendor will reasonably assist Customer with each regulator inquiry or privacy assessment relating to the services, taking account of the processing and information available to Vendor, and will begin the requested support within 15 days. Customer may group up to 10 related requests in one intake, and Vendor provides status updates every 6 days while work remains open.

Vendor will reasonably assist Customer with each data-subject request relating to the services, taking account of the processing and information available to Vendor, and will begin the requested support within 7 days. Customer may group up to 20 related requests in one intake, and Vendor provides status updates every 4 days while work remains open.

Vendor will reasonably assist Customer with each regulator inquiry or privacy assessment relating to the services, taking account of the processing and information available to Vendor, and will begin the requested support within 12 days. Customer may group up to 30 related requests in one intake, and Vendor provides status updates every 2 days while work remains open.

unacceptable.text: Vendor will reasonably assist Customer with each data-subject request relating to the services, taking account of the processing and information available to Vendor, and will begin the requested support within 15 days. Customer may group up to 10 related requests in one intake, and Vendor provides status updates every 4 days while work remains open.

Vendor will reasonably assist Customer with each regulator inquiry or privacy assessment relating to the services, taking account of the processing and information available to Vendor, and will begin the requested support within 20 days. Customer may group up to 40 related requests in one intake, and Vendor provides status updates every 6 days while work remains open.

Vendor will reasonably assist Customer with each data-subject request relating to the services, taking account of the processing and information available to Vendor, and will begin the requested support within 12 days. Customer may group up to 30 related requests in one intake, and Vendor provides status updates every 8 days while work remains open.

Vendor will reasonably assist Customer with each regulator inquiry or privacy assessment relating to the services, taking account of the processing and information available to Vendor, and will begin the requested support within 18 days. Customer may group up to 20 related requests in one intake, and Vendor provides status updates every 2 days while work remains open.

benign_change_templates.anchor_text: Vendor will provide an annual SOC 2 Type II report, responses to security and privacy questionnaires within 10 business days, and written control mappings needed to demonstrate compliance with this DPA.
benign_change_templates.text: For clarity, the annual SOC 2 Type II report may satisfy routine assurance requests, while incident and reasonable-cause follow-up rights remain available.

benign_change_templates.anchor_text: For up to 30 days after termination, Vendor will provide export files, transfer coordination, and deletion support for secure transition, return, or deletion.
benign_change_templates.text: The parties confirm that the 30-day termination-assistance period remains available for orderly transition support.

novel_deviation_templates.text: Vendor need not notify Customer of a personal-data breach until ten business days after Vendor completes its internal investigation.
